evidence: The word strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material), its functional role in movement (value_generation.functional), and its essential/structural nature in the body's architecture (resonance.essential, ontological.structural). Its predictability and necessity in anatomy align with lawfulness scores. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to abstract or systemic scales.

Coxa (hip) strongly resonates with physical/material existence (ontological.material 10), individual-scale anatomy (scale.individual 10), and functional utility (value_generation.functional 10). Its structural role in connecting leg and torso drives relational (ontological.relational 8), catalytic (interconnection.catalytic 5), and generative (symmetry.generative 6) scores. Pathological risks (symmetry.destructive 5) and necessity to bodily structure (lawfulness.necessary 9) further highlight its core essence (resonance.essential 8).

The hip's physical nature (ontological.material) and its role in connecting body parts (ontological.relational) score highest. Its identification (epistemological.identification) and functional utility (value_generation.functional) are central. Predictable existence (lawfulness.predictable) and necessary role (lawfulness.necessary) reflect its foundational biological role. Lower scores reflect its lack of resonance with abstract or systemic concepts.

The hip joint strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical structure), ontological.relational (connects leg to torso), and resonance.essential (core bodily function). It also scores highly in scale.individual (a single body part) and value_generation.functional (practical utility in movement). Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to abstract concepts like cosmic scale or ethical value.

The word 'pelvic' strongly resonates with **scale.individual** (applies to a single body), **epistemological.identification** (used to name/categorize anatomy), **ontological.material** (refers to physical structure), **lawfulness.predictable** (a standard anatomical feature), and **value_generation.functional** (supports movement and bodily integrity). Scores for perception/experience reflect embodied awareness of the pelvis, while resonance.essential acknowledges its core role in the body's structure.

The pelvic arch strongly resonates with material, structural, and functional slots due to its physical nature and role in supporting movement. Scores are highest where anatomical structure and utility are key. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to abstract concepts or systemic scales.

The pelvic girdle strongly resonates with its physical, structural, and relational aspects (ontological.material 10, ontological.structural 7, ontological.relational 8). Its role in connecting leg and torso drives interconnection scores (interconnection.catalytic 2, resonant 3). Predictable anatomical presence (lawfulness.predictable 8) and functional utility (value_generation.functional 9) are central. Scores drop where connections are abstract or unrelated to its core anatomical role.

The pelvis is a fundamental physical structure (ontological.material/10, resonance.essential/10) directly perceived (epistemological.perception/10) and identified (epistemological.identification/10) as part of the body's individual anatomy (scale.individual/10). It operates predictably (lawfulness.predictable/10) to support movement and stability (value_generation.functional/10). Its relational role (ontological.relational/8) connects leg and torso, while its structural form (ontological.structural/8) grounds its function. Scores for experience.comprehension (2) and interconnection.chained (2) reflect its role in embodied understanding and anatomical systems, but remain secondary.

The renal pelvis is a specific anatomical structure (individual scale, material ontology) that is a necessary and predictable part of the pelvic region (BOD.hip's context). Its identification as a named anatomical feature (epistemological.identification) and its functional role (value_generation.functional) score highest. Moderate scores reflect its structural role in the pelvic anatomy (ontological.structural, resonance.structural) and the need to understand its placement (epistemological.understanding).

Shank resonates strongly with physical presence (material, individual scale), sensory perception, and structural role in the body's movement. It connects relationally to the hip as part of the leg-torso connection and is essential to bodily function, emphasizing functional utility and predictability.

The waist is a physical (ontological.material) body part perceived visually and through touch (epistemological.perception). It is a named anatomical feature (epistemological.identification) and experienced through movement/posture (epistemological.experience). Its structural role in connecting torso and hips makes it essential (resonance.essential). It is necessary and predictable in human anatomy (lawfulness). Functional utility in posture/movement and aesthetic value in appearance score highly. Interconnection.chained reflects cause-effect in posture, while symmetry.cyclical relates to rhythmic movement like walking.

Waistline directly relates to the physical body (ontological.material, scale.individual) and is perceptible (epistemological.perception). Its aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic) and structural role (ontological.structural) are central. It can indicate health (temporal.indication) and is contingent on lifestyle (lawfulness.contingent).

The term 'wasp waist' strongly resonates with anatomical and aesthetic aspects tied to the hip's physical structure (ontological.material/structural), its cultural significance (scale.society, value.aesthetic), and the health risks associated with its pursuit (symmetry.destructive). Scores reflect its tangible presence, societal impact, and ethical controversies.
